message:
fix(vdbe): stable sort for COLLATE NOCASE DISTINCT + 21 conformance oracle tests

Fix #82: Sorter tie-breaker in compare_sorter_rows() broke insertion-order
stability for NOCASE-equal keys. SELECT DISTINCT on COLLATE NOCASE columns
returned the binary-first variant instead of the first-inserted value.
Removed the tie-breaker; Rust's sort_by is already stable.

Also fix pre-existing is_true_false_params_core compilation error (borrow).

Added 21 new conformance oracle test functions covering: type coercion,
COALESCE/IIF/NULLIF, compound SELECT, GROUP BY expressions, subquery
positions, JOIN varieties, CTAS, expression eval edges, UPDATE patterns,
views, NULL handling, implicit type conversion, float formatting, COLLATE
NOCASE, complex WHERE, DML complex, printf, CTE advanced, aggregate
expressions, unique constraints, multi-index queries.

All 50 oracle tests pass. 0 mismatches with C SQLite.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
